title: Kanecta Item
description: Unified schema for a Kanecta item.json file. v1.4.0 merges the separate metadata.json, function.json, type.json, and object.json files from v1.3.0 into a single file. All items share the same top-level fields; type-specific content lives under a 'payload' key.

specVersion.description: Version of the Kanecta specification this record conforms to (e.g. '1.4.0'). Set at creation time; tooling uses it to pick the right schema/migration rules when reading older records.

parentId.description: UUID of the parent item. Always required — no item is parentless. The root item is self-referencing: its id and parentId are both '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000', the well-known all-zeros UUID. Typed objects use their type item's UUID as parentId (they live in the type's collection, symlinked from wherever they appear in the tree). Free-form items use their tree parent's UUID.

value.description: Primary content or label of the item. For function items this is the function name. For type items this is the capitalised type name (e.g. 'Person').

type.description: Item type. All valid values are defined in ./types/primitive.json. Use 'function' for function items, 'type' for type definition items, 'object' for instances of a custom type.

typeId.description: UUID of the custom type definition. Required when type is 'object'. Null otherwise.

owner.description: Identifier of the individual or team responsible for this item (e.g. an email address or team alias). In a corporate context this is the person or team, not the company — use copyrightHolder for the legal rights holder.

ownerDomain.description: Domain of the owner's home Kanecta instance (e.g. 'kanecta.acme.com'). Kanecta uses this to know where to query when refreshing a cached copy of any item owned by this owner. Null for local items with no known remote home.

mirrors.description: Optional list of mirror domains advertised by the item owner (e.g. ['kanecta-us.acme.com', 'kanecta-eu.acme.com']). Consumers may fetch from any mirror but must verify the result against contentHash. ownerDomain remains the canonical authority — mirrors are fetch alternatives only. Set by the owner, not the consumer.

namespace.description: Slash-separated path expressing the organisational hierarchy this item belongs to (e.g. 'acme.com', 'acme.com/engineering', 'acme.com/engineering/platform-team'). The root segment is a domain. Used for access control scoping, display grouping, and cache routing. Null for personal or unaffiliated items.

copyrightHolder.description: Legal copyright holder for this item (e.g. 'acme.com', 'Acme Ltd'). In a corporate context this is typically the company, not the individual in owner. Null means the copyright holder is the same as owner.

contentHash.description: SHA-256 hash of the item's canonical content (all fields except contentHash itself, keys sorted, no trailing whitespace). Used by recipients to verify integrity when an item is shared or cached, and to detect staleness by comparing against the source's current hash. Null if not yet computed.

license.description: UUID reference to this item's licence. Always set — defaults to 'All Rights Reserved (Copyright)'. References a row in the fixed, shipped reference set of common licences.

sortOrder.description: Numeric sort position among siblings, scoped per aspect. Null for type definition records.

confidence.description: Confidence level of the item's content.

status.description: Arbitrary status string (e.g. 'active', 'archived', 'draft'). Null if not set.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this item was created.

modifiedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this item was last modified.

createdBy.description: Identifier of the actor who created this item.

modifiedBy.description: Identifier of the actor who last modified this item.

cachedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when remote content was last cached locally. Required for items stored in remotes/; null for local items.

subscribedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this item subscribed to a remote source.

subscriptionSource.description: URI of the remote source this item subscribes to.

completedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this item was marked as completed. Null if not completed.

dueAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this item is due. Null if no due date.

visibility.description: Coarse default access level. 'private' = owner and explicitly-granted principals only; 'organisation' = visible to anyone in the owner's organisation; 'public' = visible to anyone. Fine-grained per-principal grants layer on top.

aspect.description: Dimension this item occupies under its parent. Null = main tree (default). Any other string names an alternative dimension (e.g. 'settings', 'archive'). An item belongs to exactly one aspect. sortOrder is scoped per aspect.

template.description: UUID of the type or item this item's tree was copied from via a template action. Null if not produced by templating.

files.description: Map of semantic role names to sidecar filenames stored alongside item.json in the same item folder. Used by filesystem and filesystem+sqlite adapters. Keys are role names; values are filenames. The postgres adapter stores equivalent content inline and omits this field. Well-known roles: 'body' (source code or markdown body — e.g. 'body.ts', 'body.md'), 'image' (primary image — e.g. 'photo.jpg'), 'file' (attached file — e.g. 'report.pdf'), 'thumbnail' (preview image), 'embedding' (float32 vector binary — always 'embedding.bin'). Examples by type: function → { "body": "body.ts" }; markdown → { "body": "body.md" }; image → { "image": "photo.jpg" }; any item with an embedding → { "embedding": "embedding.bin" }.

embedding.description: Metadata describing the semantic embedding computed for this item. The float vector itself lives in a binary sidecar declared as files.embedding = 'embedding.bin' — a raw float32 array, compact and model-agnostic. Adapters read the sidecar once at load time and push it into their native vector store (pgvector for Postgres, sqlite-vec for SQLite). Sync transfers item.json + embedding.bin together; receiving nodes load the binary directly with no re-embedding required. Null if no embedding has been computed. Re-embedding is skippable when contentHash and model are both unchanged.

model.description: Embedding model identifier used to generate the vector (e.g. 'text-embedding-3-small', 'text-embedding-3-large'). Must match what the adapter uses for search — mixing models produces meaningless similarity scores.

dimensions.description: Length of the float vector (e.g. 1536 for text-embedding-3-small, 3072 for text-embedding-3-large). Stored here so adapters can validate or re-create their vector columns without reading the actual vector.

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this embedding was generated. Combined with contentHash, allows any node to decide whether to re-embed (content changed) or reuse (hash unchanged, same model).

payload.description: Type-specific content. Required for 'function' and 'type' items (see allOf conditionals below for their exact shape). Present for 'object' items as a free-form key-value object validated at runtime against the type definition's jsonSchema. Omitted or null for all other item types.

parameterOrProp.description: A typed input — used for function parameters and component props. Exactly one of type, typeId, or functionId must be set. type = TypeScript primitive; typeId = Kanecta object type instance; functionId = Kanecta function item (for passing functions/callbacks).

type.description: TypeScript primitive type only: string, number, boolean, null, undefined, void, never, unknown, any, symbol, bigint, and compositions of these. Object types must use typeId; function types must use functionId.

typeId.description: UUID of a Kanecta type definition. Use for any object type.

functionId.description: UUID of a Kanecta function item. Use when this parameter or prop expects a function/callback. The referenced function item defines the expected signature.

rest.description: Whether this is a rest parameter (...args). Functions only.

defaultValue.description: Default value expression as a source string (e.g. '0', '[]').

functionPayload.description: Payload for items of type 'function'. Captures a TypeScript-compatible function signature and optional implementation. The function name is stored in the item's value field.

description.description: JSDoc-style description of what the function does.

ai.description: Whether the function invokes AI during its internal operations.

skill.description: UUID of a Kanecta skill item. When present, the function delegates to that skill instead of (or in addition to) body.

typeParameters.description: Generic type parameters (e.g. <T extends string = string>).

constraint.description: Constraint on the type parameter (e.g. 'extends string').

parameters.description: Ordered list of function parameters. Each must have exactly one of: type (TypeScript primitive), typeId (Kanecta object type), or functionId (Kanecta function item — for passing functions/callbacks as arguments).

returnType.description: TypeScript primitive return type only. Primitives and their compositions (void, string, Promise<string>, T | null). Object types must use returnTypeId.

returnTypeId.description: UUID of a Kanecta type definition for the return value. Use for any object return type. Mutually exclusive with returnType.

throws.description: Error types this function may throw.

deprecated.description: Deprecation notice. Present and non-empty when the function is deprecated.

body.description: Function body as source code. Omit when describing a signature only. Postgres adapter stores this inline as a column value. Filesystem and filesystem+sqlite adapters omit this field and store the body as a sidecar file referenced via the top-level files map (e.g. files.body = 'body.ts').

includeKanectaSdk.description: Whether to include @kanecta/sdk in the generated scaffold.

dependencies.description: Additional npm packages for the function scaffold (e.g. ['axios@^1.0.0', 'lodash']).

typePayload.description: Payload for items of type 'type'. Defines a custom Kanecta type: its display metadata, JSON Schema for instances, and SQL DDL for storage.

details.description: Longer description: when to use this type and how it relates to others.

keywords.description: Space-separated keywords for search and filtering.

primaryField.description: The field (or computed combination of fields) that best represents an item of this type for display. Three forms: (1) a plain string field path (e.g. 'name', 'title') — simple, most common; (2) an object with a single-entry fields array — equivalent to the string form; (3) an object with multiple fields plus an expression (inline JS, args are the field values) or a functionId (UUID of a Kanecta function item) for computed display values. functionId takes precedence over expression when both are set.

oneOf.description: Dot-separated path to the primary field (e.g. 'name', 'address.street').

fields.description: Field paths whose values are passed to expression or functionId. If only one entry and no expression or functionId, behaves identically to the plain string form.

expression.description: Inline JS expression returning the display string. Field values are in scope by their field name (e.g. 'firstName + " " + lastName'). Used for simple computed values without a full function item.

functionId.description: UUID of a Kanecta function item that accepts the listed fields as arguments and returns the display string. Takes precedence over expression when both are set.

claude.description: Guidance for Claude on when and how to use this type.

componentId.description: UUID of a Kanecta component item that renders instances of this type. The UI passes the instance's payload fields as props. Null if no component is registered.

functions.description: UUIDs of function items associated with this type.

sync.description: UUIDs of function items that can sync instances of this type from an external source.

supersededBy.description: UUIDs of type definitions that replace this one.

implements.description: UUIDs of types whose contract this type fulfils (interface-style, not storage inheritance).

extends.description: UUIDs of types this type extends/specialises (declared relationship, not storage inheritance).

immutable.description: When true, the type's contract (jsonSchema, sqlSchema, meta.primaryField) is sealed. A sealed type needing a contract change must become a new type with a new UUID.

hash.description: Hex-encoded SHA-256 over the contract fields (jsonSchema + sqlSchema + meta.primaryField, keys sorted). Set when immutable is true; re-checked on load to detect tampering.

jsonSchema.description: JSON Schema Draft-07 definition for instances of this type. Types are flat — exactly one level deep. Each property must carry a stable 'x-id' UUID. Properties must be primitives, arrays of primitives, or typeId references to other Kanecta types — no inline nested objects.

$id.description: Canonical URI for this type schema.

properties.description: Field definitions. Each property must include 'x-id' with a stable UUID.

sqlSchema.description: Ordered SQL DDL statements (PostgreSQL dialect) that, when run against a database with the items table, create all storage for instances of this type. Defined once at creation; never altered (a changed shape is a new type). Table name convention: obj_<type-uuid-with-underscores>.

relationshipPayload.description: Payload for items of type 'relationship'. A typed, first-class relationship between two items. Coexists with the lighter-weight inline [[uuid]] links and relationships.json mechanism from v1.3.0 — both are valid. Use relationship items when you need a named type, direction control, or the relationship itself needs to be annotated, tagged, or linked. The optional label lives in the item's value field.

relationshipType.description: The kind of relationship. Free-form string — implementations may define their own. Standard types: 'relates-to', 'depends-on', 'enables', 'contradicts', 'blocks', 'blocked-by', 'prerequisite-for', 'derived-from', 'supersedes'.

sourceId.description: UUID of the source item — the item the relationship originates from.

targetId.description: UUID of the target item — the item the relationship points to.

direction.description: Whether the relationship has a direction. 'directed' = sourceId → targetId only. 'bidirectional' = the relationship is symmetric and sourceId/targetId are interchangeable.

grantPayload.description: Payload for items of type 'grant'. Grants live as children of the item they control access to, in the 'grants' aspect. principal supports three forms: a direct identity string, a namespace path, or a relationship reference (any principal that holds a given relation to a given item). The third form enables relationship-based access control — groups are ordinary Kanecta items, membership is a Kanecta relationship, and grant resolution is a graph traversal.

principal.description: Who receives this grant. Three forms: (1) identity string — a direct user or system (e.g. 'bob@acme.com'); (2) namespace string — everyone in an organisational scope (e.g. 'acme.com/engineering'); (3) relation object — any principal that holds the named relation to the named item (e.g. members of a group item). Form is detected by type: string = identity or namespace, object = relation.

oneOf.description: Direct identity (e.g. 'bob@acme.com') or namespace path (e.g. 'acme.com/engineering'). Namespace paths match the principal and all sub-paths.
Relationship-based principal. Grants access to any principal that holds the named relation to the named item — e.g. anyone who is a 'member' of a group item.

itemId.description: UUID of the Kanecta item that principals are related to (e.g. a group, team, or role item).

relation.description: The relationship type the principal must hold to itemId (e.g. 'member', 'owner', 'subscriber').

permissions.description: Permissions granted to the principal. 'read' = view the item and its main-aspect children; 'write' = create, update, or delete the item and its children; 'subscribe' = cache this item and receive updates; 'admin' = manage grants on this item.

cascade.description: When true, this grant applies to all descendants of the item, not just the item itself. Resolved by traversing the tree and applying the grant to each child unless overridden by a more specific grant.

componentPayload.description: Payload for items of type 'component'. Describes a React component: its props interface (using the shared parameterOrProp definition, same as function parameters) and optional npm dependencies. The component body (JSX/TSX) lives in a sidecar file referenced via files.body = 'body.tsx'. Return type is always ReactNode — implicit, no field needed. A type item can reference a component via payload.meta.componentId, which tells the UI to render instances of that type using this component.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this component renders.

props.description: The component's prop interface. Each prop follows the same parameterOrProp definition as function parameters — primitive type, typeId (Kanecta type), or functionId (Kanecta function item, for callback props).

dependencies.description: npm package names this component imports beyond React itself (e.g. ['recharts', 'date-fns']).

examples._comment: function item — filesystem/sqlite adapter (body as sidecar)

payload.description: Fetch a user by ID from the API.
Fetch a user by ID from the API.

meta.description: A human individual as a biographical fact.

sqlSchema:
CREATE TABLE "obj_f1e2d3c4_b5a6_7890_abcd_ef1234567890" (
    item_id UUID NOT NULL,
    "name" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "born" DATE,
    CONSTRAINT "pk_obj_f1e2d3c4_b5a6_7890_abcd_ef1234567890" PRIMARY KEY (item_id),
    CONSTRAINT "fk_obj_f1e2d3c4_b5a6_7890_abcd_ef1234567890_item" FOREIGN KEY (item_id) REFERENCES items(id)
)
